message.content:
Search the most recent transcript files in `C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\` for any planned work or draft. The user says "did you forget the draft that we planned earlier?" — they're referring to a plan from a DIFFERENT session, not the reaction labels plan.

First, list the transcript .jsonl files (NOT in subagent folders) sorted by modification time to find the most recent ones. Then search the most recent 3-5 transcripts for any discussion about planned features, improvements, or next steps.

Search terms to try: "draft", "plan", "next", "implement", "feature", "phase", "TODO", "improvement", "enhance", "add", "build".

Also check if there are any plan files in `C:\Users\johnm\.claude\plans\` besides `jolly-splashing-shore.md`.

content.text: I'll help you find the planned work from earlier sessions. Let me start by listing the transcript files and checking for any plan files.

input.description: List JSONL transcript files sorted by modification time
List plan files in the plans directory

content.text: Now let me search the 5 most recent transcripts for planned work. I'll grep for key terms like "draft", "plan", "next", "implement", "feature", "TODO":

input.description: Peek at the most recent transcript structure

content.text: The transcripts are JSONL format. Let me search more efficiently using the proper structure. I'll look at the most recent several transcripts for planning discussion:

input.command: for file in d64f1ce5-0039-4323-84b8-a490c87f5a3e.jsonl 85403f76-9c6f-4f2d-a39c-a25a5b9179ec.jsonl cfec12d8-10aa-428b-a19d-fd2c2d27deda.jsonl 7d89beac-b62f-40b4-a937-54714dd41672.jsonl 28ab47f1-547a-4bbb-9bb8-9f9c380ddc1d.jsonl; do echo "=== $file ==="; grep -io 'draft\|plan\|next\|implement\|feature\|TODO\|improvement\|enhance' "C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3/$file" | sort | uniq -c | head -3; done
input.description: Search for plan-related keywords in 5 most recent transcripts
